constraint_form: rollout-evaluated safety metrics and local smoothness-based safe regions in parameter space.
guarantee_basis: sampling-based local safe region and projected policy-update analysis.
inference_dependency: no output repair layer; safety is enforced during parameter updates.
tradeoff: provides training-time safe-update guarantees for policy learning, but depends on rollout coverage, local smoothness bounds, feasible QCQP projections, and a safe initialization.

constraint_form: constraints whose feasible set is homeomorphic to a unit ball.
guarantee_basis: mapping validity and latent bisection under the paper's assumptions.
tradeoff: effectiveness depends on learning a valid low-distortion homeomorphism.

constraint_form: constrained sets satisfying the paper's homeomorphism assumptions.
guarantee_basis: homeomorphism/bi-Lipschitz assumptions and bisection construction.
tradeoff: requires a learned map that accurately represents the feasible set geometry.

constraint_form: compact input-dependent feasible sets with non-empty interiors under the paper's assumptions; experiments include convex and nonconvex constrained optimization instances.
guarantee_basis: sufficient conditions for IPNN feasibility, compact-set/interior assumptions, feasibility checks, and bisection analysis.
inference_dependency: raw predictor, IPNN, and finite bisection steps.
tradeoff: lower online cost than generic projection, but performance depends on valid interior-point prediction, feasible-set membership checks, and the number of bisection steps.

constraint_form: equality and inequality constraints in hard-constrained optimization.
guarantee_basis: analytical completion plus iterative correction; tolerance and problem structure matter.
tradeoff: stronger feasibility handling requires extra inference-time correction.

constraint_form: linear, convex quadratic, second-order cone, linear matrix inequality, and related convex constraints stated by the paper.
guarantee_basis: by-construction parameterization described by the paper.
tradeoff: applicability follows the supported convex constraint classes.

constraint_form: homogeneous linear inequalities `Ax <= 0`.
guarantee_basis: by-construction feasible parameterization.
inference_dependency: none beyond the constrained network layer.
tradeoff: limited to the homogeneous linear inequality setting in this first pass.

constraint_form: finite-horizon MPC constraints with polytopic state/input constraints in the paper's studied settings.
guarantee_basis: Phase-I interior-point construction and gauge-map parameterization of the MPC feasible set.
inference_dependency: interior-point construction and closed-form gauge-map evaluation.
tradeoff: feasibility depends on the MPC feasible set being strictly feasible and on the availability and stability of the gauge-map construction; the approach targets speed compared with online MPC or projection-based correction.

constraint_form: convex and nonconvex constraints in the paper's experiments.
guarantee_basis: projection/constrained optimization during sampling.
inference_dependency: projection or constrained solve during sampling.
tradeoff: stronger constraint handling adds optimization cost and depends on solver quality.

constraint_form: constrained optimization problems.
guarantee_basis: empirical feasibility and optimality-gap evaluation; no by-construction pointwise feasibility of the raw primal network is claimed.
inference_dependency: feed-forward primal and dual networks, with training inspired by ALM updates.
tradeoff: avoids presolved labels and uses instance-specific multipliers, but feasibility remains measured by violations unless paired with an additional repair/certificate.

constraint_form: statistical/physical consistency constraints between low-resolution inputs and high-resolution outputs.
guarantee_basis: by-construction terminal renormalization layer.
inference_dependency: constraint layer evaluation after the base neural model.
tradeoff: applies when the input-output relationship admits known aggregate/statistical constraints.

constraint_form: linear and nonlinear conic optimization problems.
guarantee_basis: conic dual completion and projection.
inference_dependency: dual proxy plus completion/projection formulas or layers.
tradeoff: provides certificates/bounds but must be paired with a primal proxy if a feasible primal decision is needed.

constraint_form: convex approximations of energy-system operational constraints.
guarantee_basis: projection onto the modeled convex set.
tradeoff: feasibility is with respect to approximate convex constraints, so model fidelity matters.

constraint_form: AC-OPF power-flow constraints formulated through a relaxed QCQP.
guarantee_basis: differentiable QCQP activation and constraint-violation loss; exact hard-feasibility scope should be checked against the theorem/algorithm section before final claims.
tradeoff: stronger AC structure handling adds a specialized nonconvex QCQP component.

constraint_form: smooth equality and inequality constraints.
guarantee_basis: correction plus active-set-inspired boundary wandering; paper states convergence/theoretical guarantees for BWS.
tradeoff: additional correction/search steps improve feasibility-objective balance but add inference complexity.

constraint_form: equality and inequality constraints, including convex and nonconvex cases covered experimentally.
guarantee_basis: feasibility-seeking optimization and stated boundedness/smoothness/PL-condition analysis.
inference_dependency: iterative feasibility-seeking solve after neural prediction.
tradeoff: general feasibility handling requires unrolled iterative correction at training and inference.

constraint_form: linear equalities and inequalities transformed into inequality form.
guarantee_basis: SKM-type iterative feasibility theory and the paper's post-processing analysis.
tradeoff: targets linear constraints and introduces iterative inference cost.

constraint_form: bounded and general linear constraints.
guarantee_basis: convex dual reformulation and accelerated gradient convergence.
tradeoff: general linear support is obtained through iterative optimization rather than a closed-form map.

constraint_form: convex constraints over variable-size graph outputs.
guarantee_basis: CAD best-approximation convergence for the modeled convex sets.
tradeoff: exact projection gradients are replaced by a surrogate gradient for training efficiency.

constraint_form: convex constraints.
guarantee_basis: orthogonal projection onto the convex feasible set.
tradeoff: feasible outputs require a projection solve; accuracy and speed depend on projection convergence.

constraint_form: nonlinear equality constraints depending on inputs and outputs.
guarantee_basis: adaptive projection construction and stated Lipschitz/stability analysis.
tradeoff: focused on equality constraints, with computational complexity tied to the number of constraints.

constraint_form: input-dependent constraints, including nonconvex cases in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: differentiable repair iterations and adaptive relaxation framework.
tradeoff: broader constraint handling depends on iterative repair precision and tolerance settings.

constraint_form: integer constraints plus continuous feasibility constraints in MINLPs.
guarantee_basis: correction-layer construction and projection convergence proof.
inference_dependency: integer correction layers plus projection step.
tradeoff: general MINLP coverage requires correction/projection logic after neural prediction.

constraint_form: operational/physical constraints, including nonlinear constraints in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: differentiable probabilistic projection layer.
tradeoff: projection-layer design must match the target constraints and forecasting distributional setup.

constraint_form: feasibility constraints from inverse-problem measurement models.
guarantee_basis: nonexpansive operator/fixed-point formulation.
tradeoff: feasibility is tied to the modeled projection operators, while recovery quality depends on learned regularization.

constraint_form: data-manifold constraint/regularization.
guarantee_basis: Wasserstein projection learning analysis.
inference_dependency: learned projection inside an iterative inverse-problem method.
tradeoff: guarantees are for approximating the data-manifold projection, not arbitrary task constraints.

method_family: gauge-based feasible mapping for power dispatch.
constraint_form: linear constraints in power dispatch.
guarantee_basis: gauge-map construction over the constrained domain.
tradeoff: depends on availability and numerical stability of the generalized gauge construction for the target linear domain.

constraint_form: convex constraints including linear, quadratic, equality, dynamic, and boundary-form constraints in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: by-construction feasible mapping layer.
tradeoff: scope and computational complexity depend on the supported constraint types.

constraint_form: star-shaped hard constraints.
guarantee_basis: ray visibility and differentiable boundary-search construction.
tradeoff: requires a valid star-shaped origin and boundary/ray-intersection routine.

constraint_form: input-dependent linear equalities and inequalities.
guarantee_basis: decision-rule feasible safe network and convex-combination construction.
tradeoff: guarantee is tied to tractable decision-rule formulations for the specified linear constraints.

constraint_form: linear optimization dual constraints.
guarantee_basis: dual completion optimization and closed-form completion formulas for covered penalty classes.
tradeoff: provides dual certificates rather than direct primal feasibility; primal decisions still require a compatible primal method.

method_family: closed-form hard constraint enforcement layer for the HardNet-Aff setting.
constraint_form: multiple input-dependent affine constraints, including linear inequalities with input-dependent bounds; the paper's broader HardNet-Cvx discussion is optimization-based rather than the closed-form HardNet-Aff layer.
guarantee_basis: closed-form HardNet-Aff enforcement layer and universal-approximation analysis.
tradeoff: closed-form guarantees apply to the affine/linear HardNet-Aff scope; broader convex constraints require the paper's optimization-based HardNet-Cvx construction.

constraint_form: arbitrary-cardinality input-dependent affine inequality constraints.
guarantee_basis: closed-form construction and universal-approximation analysis under the paper's assumptions.
tradeoff: scope remains affine; the method extends HardNet-Aff cardinality/rank limitations rather than handling general nonlinear constraints.

method_family: differentiable projection layer for LMI constraints.
constraint_form: parameterized linear matrix inequalities \(F_0(\xi)+\sum_i y_i F_i(\xi)\succeq 0\).
guarantee_basis: Douglas--Rachford convergence under the stated assumptions.
tradeoff: expands feasibility layers to semidefinite/LMI constraints but introduces splitting iterations and tolerance-dependent feasibility.

constraint_form: nonlinear equality and inequality constraints with lower/upper bounds \(b_l(x)\le c_x(y)\le b_u(x)\).
guarantee_basis: convergence analysis for the iterative local-linearization procedure.
inference_dependency: finite or converged iterative correction layer.
tradeoff: handles broader nonlinear constraints than closed-form affine layers, but feasibility is convergence/tolerance-dependent and iteration cost must be reported.

constraint_form: action constraints derived from next-step safety in convex polytopes.
guarantee_basis: convex-combination representation of the safe action polytope.
tradeoff: requires tractable vertex representation of the relevant safe set.

constraint_form: input-output specifications organized into compatible constraint sets.
guarantee_basis: correct-by-construction constrained predictors and safe convex mixing.
tradeoff: requires decomposing specifications into compatible sets and designing predictors for them.

constraint_form: discrete/combinatorial constraints such as cardinality and matroid-style feasible sets in examples.
guarantee_basis: convex-geometric decomposition over feasible polytope corners.
tradeoff: requires access to geometric/decomposition routines for the target combinatorial constraint family.

constraint_form: compact convex or nonconvex output constraint sets, plus soft constraint families in the second theorem.
guarantee_basis: constrained universal approximation theorem.
tradeoff: primarily a representation theorem; deployment as a concrete feasibility layer requires an implementable construction for the target set.

mechanism: gauge map from virtual action to safe action.
constraint_form: state and action safety constraints for linear system models with bounded disturbances.
guarantee_basis: set-theoretic control, RCI polytope construction, and valid gauge-map parameterization of the safe-action set.
tradeoff: safety guarantee depends on the computed RCI, linear model assumptions, disturbance set, and validity of the gauge map for the safe-action set.

constraint_form: hard linear constraints.
guarantee_basis: reduction, gauge mapping, and reconstruction sequence.
tradeoff: applies to hard linear constraints where the reduced feasible set and gauge function are available.

constraint_form: compact convex sets, with some nonconvex extensions in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: homeomorphic transformation and convergence-rate analysis.
tradeoff: this is an optimization algorithm rather than a trained prediction layer; it needs a valid homeomorphism for the target set.

constraint_form: star-convex hard constraints.
guarantee_basis: polar homeomorphism and boundary-distance construction.
inference_dependency: polar homeomorphic map and boundary-distance calculation.
tradeoff: requires a valid polar center and boundary-distance routine; training can require reconnection strategies to avoid polar-coordinate stagnation.

constraint_form: constraints whose feasible set supports the radial interior map described by the paper.
guarantee_basis: by-construction radial interior map and full-rank-Jacobian analysis almost everywhere.
tradeoff: interior feasibility can trade off boundary reach, and applicability depends on a valid radial map.

constraint_form: complex operational constraints, including nonconvex examples in the paper.
guarantee_basis: amortized learned projection; constraint satisfaction should be empirically validated for the target problem.
tradeoff: much cheaper than solver-based correction, but accuracy depends on learned feasible-set representation.

constraint_form: arbitrary compact convex sets, with extensions to star-convex and geodesic-convex sets.
guarantee_basis: bijective gauge construction, bounded distribution-approximation analysis, and stated nonconvex extensions.
inference_dependency: gauge map, ball-domain sampler operations, and inverse map.
tradeoff: constraint coverage depends on a valid gauge/bijective construction for the target geometry.

constraint_form: nonconvex equality and inequality constraints.
guarantee_basis: landing mechanism in constrained stochastic dynamics.
tradeoff: feasibility and efficiency depend on the landing construction and the cost of constrained dynamics.

constraint_form: bridge-form constraints that can be manually specified and combined.
guarantee_basis: mathematical validity of the manual-bridge construction and constraint-combination rules.
tradeoff: requires constraints to be expressible through the manual-bridge framework.

constraint_form: time-series hard constraints, including many simultaneous constraints in experiments.
guarantee_basis: projection step applied during posterior sampling; tolerance and projection quality matter.
tradeoff: repeated projection improves feasibility but adds sampling-time correction cost.

constraint_form: bonding, stereochemical, geometric, motif, and pocket-design constraints in protein design.
guarantee_basis: proximal feasibility updates and ADMM decomposition in the generative process.
inference_dependency: feasibility-update and ADMM correction steps during sampling.
tradeoff: domain-specific constraint handling increases sampling/correction complexity.

constraint_form: arbitrary constraint sets in the paper's stated stable-diffusion framework, with convex-constraint guarantees discussed.
guarantee_basis: proximal Langevin correction and projection/surrogate constraint evaluation.
inference_dependency: iterative latent correction during sampling; surrogate or simulator if constraints are not directly differentiable.
tradeoff: training-free and compatible with stable diffusion, but correction quality depends on decoder gradients and constraint/surrogate fidelity.

constraint_form: proxy-constraint functions used during diffusion inference.
guarantee_basis: trust-region/proxy-guidance heuristic; first-pass source does not support a hard pointwise guarantee.
inference_dependency: multiple proxy-gradient steps and manifold trust checks during sampling.
tradeoff: flexible and training-free, but guidance strength and trust criteria affect sample quality and feasibility.

constraint_form: trajectory feasibility as adherence to the data/manifold of valid offline trajectories.
guarantee_basis: local manifold approximation and projection; feasibility is data-manifold dependent.
inference_dependency: nearest-neighbor retrieval, local PCA, and projection at sampling steps.
tradeoff: training-free but adds retrieval/projection cost and depends on offline dataset coverage.

constraint_form: measurement-space residual constraints for inverse problems.
guarantee_basis: hard residual projection plus local descent/backtracking analysis.
inference_dependency: fixed-budget correction iterations using operator evaluations and autodiff primitives.
tradeoff: efficient for inverse problems, but guarantees are local/per-step and tied to the measurement model and residual budget.

constraint_form: sequence-level constraints and logic rules over discrete outputs.
guarantee_basis: sampling-time differentiable constraint optimization.
inference_dependency: constrained discrete diffusion correction during sampling.
tradeoff: training-free constraint insertion adds sampling-time optimization and depends on the constraint optimizer.

constraint_form: constraints handled by projection-based constrained sampling.
guarantee_basis: training-sampling alignment plus projection-based inference; pointwise feasibility comes from the projection procedure, not the loss alone.
tradeoff: improves alignment but requires training with the intended projection mechanism.

constraint_form: measurement consistency and data-manifold constraints for inverse problems.
guarantee_basis: correction term analysis and empirical validation; not a standalone hard feasibility guarantee.
tradeoff: simple to attach, but feasibility depends on the base solver and correction quality.

constraint_form: terminal constraints/manifold constraints.
guarantee_basis: optimal-control analysis; as penalty vanishes, the terminal law approaches a generalized Wasserstein projection onto the constraint manifold.
tradeoff: adds optimal-control guidance cost and relies on terminal constraint geometry.

constraint_form: nonlinear equality and inequality constraints.
guarantee_basis: exponential convergence in 2-Wasserstein distance under stated regularity assumptions.
tradeoff: rigorous guarantees require the paper's regularity assumptions and sampler discretization choices.

method_family: projection-analogous correction / bisection-based projection for chance-constrained OPF.
constraint_form: chance constraints in AC-OPF with specified confidence level.
guarantee_basis: bisection-based projection and the paper's feasibility/optimality analysis.
tradeoff: feasibility comes from the projection stage, and sampling/projection adds inference cost.

method_family: differentiable projection layer for multi-period DC-OPF.
constraint_form: DC-OPF constraints with power balance, generator limits, ramping limits, energy-storage dynamics, and line limits.
guarantee_basis: exact solution of the projection layer over the modeled feasible set and KKT-based differentiation assumptions.
tradeoff: strict feasibility for modeled linear constraints comes with projection-layer runtime and numerical differentiation cost; the scope is application-specific to multi-period DC-OPF.

constraint_form: disjunctive constraints, logical rules, QF-LRA-style linear logic, and MILP-equivalent bounded rules.
guarantee_basis: DNF convex-hull LP formulation and exactness theorem.
tradeoff: DNF convexification can grow exponentially in interacting active rules; CNF relaxations are more scalable but weaker.

constraint_form: linear constraints/polyhedral projection sets.
guarantee_basis: projection layer plus HS-Jacobian conservative-map analysis.
tradeoff: targets linear/polyhedral constraints and focuses on efficient backpropagation through nonsmooth projections.

guarantee_basis: source checked at abstract/first-pages level; mechanism details require follow-up reading for final manuscript claims.
